script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Morning, Sarah.
script.ja: おはようございます、サラさん。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Morning, Alex.
script.ja: おはよう、アレックス。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: It's Saturday, June 21st, 2026. Can you believe it? The Daily Tanu-chan Show, already weekend.
script.ja: 2026年6月21日土曜日です。信じられますか？ザ・デイリー・たぬちゃんショー、もう週末ですよ。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It is. You look like the weekend hasn't caught up to you yet.
script.ja: ええ、そうね。あなた、まだ週末に追いついてないみたいね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: No. Baby was up at three. Then five. Coffee is absolutely non-negotiable this morning.
script.ja: ええ、そうなんです。赤ちゃんが3時に起きて。それから5時にも。今朝はコーヒーが絶対必要です。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: That's normal. It ends around two.
script.ja: それは普通よ。2歳くらいで終わるわ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Two what? Years?
script.ja: 2歳？年ですか？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Yes, years. Mine did the same. Just hang in there.
script.ja: ええ、年。うちの子もそうだったわ。頑張って。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: I'm trying. Oh, hey, speaking of the day, I saw the weather forecast earlier.
script.ja: 頑張ります。あ、そうそう、今日のことですが、さっき天気予報を見ました。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Oh?
script.ja: あら？

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Yeah, it's overcast today. High around 27 degrees, low 20. And a 70% chance of rain. So no park for us.
script.ja: ええ、今日は曇り。最高気温が27度くらいで最低が20度。そして降水確率70%。だから、公園はなしですね。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: That's a shame. Good for staying inside with tea, though.
script.ja: 残念ね。でも、お茶を飲んで家で過ごすのにはいいわね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: I guess. Anyway, I saw something else about June 21st, too.
script.ja: そうですね。ところで、6月21日について、他にも何か見かけました。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Oh?
script.ja: あら？

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Yeah, it was on this day in 2009 that Greenland assumed self-rule.
script.ja: ええ、2009年の今日、グリーンランドが自治権を獲得したそうです。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Right, they got more autonomy from Denmark then.
script.ja: そうね、その時デンマークからより多くの自治権を得たのよね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: I didn't even know it wasn't self-ruling before. I guess it's good to have local control. Though I wonder what the main *prerequisite* for governing a place like that would be. Just... ice?
script.ja: それまで自治していなかったなんて知りませんでした。地方の統制があるのは良いことだと思います。でも、あのような場所を統治するための主要な『prerequisite』は何なんだろう？ただの…氷？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: A 'prerequisite' is something you absolutely need *before* something else can happen. Like a necessary condition. For Greenland, maybe good resource management skills.
script.ja: 『prerequisite』というのは、何かが起こる『前に』絶対に必要となるものよ。必要な条件、という感じ。グリーンランドなら、優れた資源管理能力かしらね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Good resource management skills. Makes sense. Speaking of things I'm learning, I was reading that 'Zero to Three' article you mentioned.
script.ja: 優れた資源管理能力。なるほど。学ぶことといえば、サラさんが話していた『Zero to Three』の記事を読んでいました。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Ah, the tips on learning to talk.
script.ja: ああ、言葉を学ぶためのヒントね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Yeah. My son is one year old this month, and he just babbles. 'Mama,' 'dada,' but not really meaning it. Is that normal? The article said by two years, they have a large vocabulary.
script.ja: ええ。息子は今月1歳になったんですが、ただ喃語を言うだけで。『ママ』とか『ダダ』とか、でも意味はなさそうで。これって普通ですか？記事には2歳までに語彙が豊富になるって書いてありました。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Perfectly normal. They're just exploring sounds at first. Mine did that too.
script.ja: 全く普通よ。最初はただ音を探っているだけ。うちの子もそうだったわ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: It feels like I'm stuck in the *doldrums* of baby talk sometimes, you know? Just repeating sounds.
script.ja: なんだか、赤ちゃんの言葉の『doldrums』にはまっているような気がするんです、分かりますか？ただ音を繰り返してるだけで。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: 'Doldrums' is a good word for that feeling. It means a period of no activity, or a feeling of being stuck and a bit low.
script.ja: 『doldrums』はその気持ちにぴったりの言葉ね。活動がない期間、あるいは行き詰まって少し気が滅入る気持ちという意味よ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Yeah, exactly. Like I'm just waiting for the wind to pick up, for him to actually say something meaningful.
script.ja: ええ、まさにそんな感じです。風が吹くのを待っているというか、彼が本当に意味のあることを言うのを待っているというか。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: He will. And then you'll wonder why he can't be quiet for five minutes.
script.ja: 言うようになるわ。そして、そのうちどうして5分間静かにできないのかしら、と思うようになるわよ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: (Laughs softly) I guess so. Thanks, Sarah. That's good to hear.
script.ja: （小さく笑う）そうかもしれませんね。ありがとう、サラさん。それを聞いて安心しました。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Anytime, Alex. Enjoy your coffee.
script.ja: どういたしまして、アレックス。コーヒーを楽しんでね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: You too. Have a good weekend, everyone.
script.ja: サラさんも。皆さん、良い週末を。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: See you Monday.
script.ja: 月曜日に会いましょう。

parenting.title: Tips on Learning to Talk
parenting.summary:
This article gives a brief introduction to language development and includes age-appropriate activities for language-building for children birth to 3 years old. Learning to talk is a process that starts at birth, when your baby experiences how voices can sound. By 2 years old, most babies have a large vocabulary and can put words together to express their needs and […]
